summary: SKPath.Op() with SKPathOp.Intersect returns a result path with 0 points for specific coordinate values and mixed fill types (Winding + EvenOdd), while the same operation works correctly in the Skia C++ fiddle.

stepsToReproduce: Create path_a with Winding fill type using hex float coordinates forming a rectangle
Create path_b with EvenOdd fill type using hex float coordinates forming a nearly-identical rectangle
Check path_r.PointCount — result is 0 instead of expected intersection points

repoLinks.description: Skia C++ fiddle demonstrating correct intersection result
Skia discussion group thread about path operations (linked in comment June 2025)

codeSnippets:
SKPath path_r = path_a.Op(path_b, SKPathOp.Intersect);
Console.WriteLine(path_r.PointCount); // prints 0

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: Issue was filed with 1.60.3 in 2018 and labeled status/skia-update-required. The 2025 comment suggests ongoing community interest; whether Skia's pathops has been fixed in subsequent updates is unknown without running the repro.

analysis.summary: The SkiaSharp C# wrapper correctly delegates path boolean operations to the native sk_pathop_op C API. The bug is in Skia's SkPathOps C++ implementation, which fails to compute the intersection of two rectangles when they use different fill types (Winding vs EvenOdd) and involve near-zero floating-point coordinate values.
analysis.rationale: The C# wrapper at SKPath.Op() is structurally sound — it validates arguments and delegates to sk_pathop_op. The P/Invoke binding is generated and correct. The discrepancy (C++ fiddle works, C# returns 0 points) isolates the bug to the native Skia pathops library. The existing label status/skia-update-required confirms prior maintainer assessment. The issue is medium severity: it produces wrong output but only for specific coordinate combinations; many path operations work correctly.

keySignals.text: The same example in C++ is working well. You can try it in the next fiddle record.
keySignals.interpretation: The C# binding is correctly passing through to native code. Since C++ works in Skia directly, the issue is in how SkiaSharp's bundled Skia version handles this pathop, not the C# wrapper.

keySignals.text: Result path contains no points. It writes 0 on console.
keySignals.interpretation: The Op() call succeeds (returns non-null) but the result path has 0 points — wrong-output rather than a crash or null return.
Mixed fill types (Winding for path_a, EvenOdd for path_b) combined with near-zero float coordinates (e.g., 0xB6D27CA9 ≈ -1.6e-8) may trigger a degenerate case in Skia's boolean ops.
Maintainers previously identified this as a Skia upstream bug awaiting a fix in a Skia update.

codeInvestigation.finding: Op() method validates non-null arguments and delegates to SkiaApi.sk_pathop_op(Handle, other.Handle, op, result.Handle). The wrapper is correct — there is no bug here.

sk_pathop_op is a generated P/Invoke binding to the native sk_pathop_op C function. The signature matches (sk_path_t* one, sk_path_t* two, sk_pathop_t op, sk_path_t* result). No issues with the binding.

nextQuestions: Is this still reproducible with the current SkiaSharp (3.x) version?
Did Skia's SkPathOps fix the mixed fill type intersection issue in a subsequent milestone?
Does the issue reproduce on Linux/macOS or only Windows?

resolution.hypothesis: Skia's SkPathOps algorithm has a degenerate case with near-zero coordinates combined with mixed Winding/EvenOdd fill types. This was a known upstream Skia bug (status/skia-update-required). Whether it has been fixed in more recent Skia milestones needs verification.

proposals.title: Verify repro with current SkiaSharp version
proposals.description: Run the reporter's code against the current SkiaSharp release. If it now works, close as fixed. If still failing, escalate to Skia upstream.

proposals.title: Workaround: convert both paths to Winding before intersection
proposals.description: Call path_b.ToWinding() to normalize fill types before performing the intersection operation. Mixing fill types in boolean ops can trigger degenerate cases in older Skia versions.

resolution.recommendedProposal: Verify repro with current SkiaSharp version
resolution.recommendedReason: Fastest path to resolution. Issue is 7+ years old and labeled as requiring a Skia update — the fix may already be in shipping versions.

actionability.reason: The bug is in the native Skia pathops library. The issue is 7+ years old with a status/skia-update-required label. Need to verify if still reproducible with current SkiaSharp before deciding to close or escalate.

actions.description: Confirm existing labels are correct (type/bug, area/libSkiaSharp.native); add tenet/reliability
Request confirmation whether this is still reproducible with current SkiaSharp, and suggest fill-type normalization workaround
